signals.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire Extended, Easing Near-Term Inflation Pressures | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US-Iran Ceasefire Extended, Easing Near-Term Inflation Pressures | YES | HIGH confidence**

The US-Iran ceasefire has been extended as of April 22, 2026[2], which directly impacts inflation trajectories after the conflict drove headline CPI to 3.29% year-over-year in March—a spike primarily fueled by energy prices that surged 10.9% monthly, with gasoline jumping 21.2%[1][3]. signals.raw:
